name: Ideation & Brainstorming
description: The designer provides a design challenge, problem statement, or brief and works iteratively with an LLM to generate candidate concepts, directions, and ideas across a broad solution space. AI produces rapid divergent output; the designer evaluates, selects, and develops promising directions. The value is in expanding ideation breadth early in the concept phase - surfacing directions the designer might not have considered - while compressing the time from problem framing to initial concept shortlist. A secondary pattern is cold-start facilitation: when the practitioner does not yet know the domain well enough to begin, AI-generated directions provide scaffolding that makes the first iteration possible.

value_hypothesis.sentence: Expands the solution space rapidly at the start of concept development, surfacing a wider range of directions than solo or small-group brainstorming while reducing the friction of blank-page ideation.

value_indicator: - Concept breadth (Innovation): number of distinct concept directions generated per session relative to solo brainstorming baseline - Ideation time (Velocity): elapsed time to initial concept shortlist relative to team workshop baseline
citations: [TBD - Source Registry not yet created] Sources: Fanny, Cattapan, Muehlhaus, GenUI study, Noltenius, Wen (Anthropic)

name: Wireframe & Layout Generation
description: The designer specifies page objectives, content requirements, or user flows, and an AI tool proposes structure and hierarchy, potential layouts, and component hierarchies. The designer evaluates proposed layouts critically - testing them against user goals, business requirements, interaction logic, and design system constraints - selecting, adapting, or rejecting AI proposals. The Challenger arrangement reflects the asymmetry of the UC: AI produces candidate layouts with structural specificity, and the designer's primary contribution is interrogating those proposals rather than generating from scratch.

value_hypothesis.sentence: Accelerates the transition from content requirements to evaluated layout structures, generating candidate wireframes for designer critique rather than requiring manual construction of each layout option.

value_indicator: - Cycle time compression (Velocity): elapsed time from content specification to evaluated wireframe shortlist relative to manual wireframing baseline - Compliance pass rate (Quality): proportion of AI-generated layout proposals conforming to design system constraints without requiring manual correction
citations: [TBD - Source Registry not yet created] Sources: NNg Tools, Figma (7 UCs), Noltenius, GenUI study

name: Prompt-to-Prototype and Vibe Coding
description: The designer describes interaction behaviors, user flows, or screen states in natural language, and an AI coding assistant generates functional prototype code. Designer and AI alternate: the designer evaluates the result in browser or preview, identifies gaps or refinements, and re-prompts. Unlike static wireframing, this approach produces interactive, clickable prototypes suitable for stakeholder review and user testing without requiring engineering resource. The workflow also extends to late-stage frontend polish - refining visual detail directly in a code editor - where practitioners report a material reduction in implementation work as a share of total time. The Co-creator arrangement reflects genuine back-and-forth: substantive creative and structural decisions made by the designer shape what the AI builds next.

value_hypothesis.sentence: Enables designers to produce functional, interactive prototypes without engineering support, unlocking prototype fidelity levels previously gated by development resource availability.

value_indicator: - New method adoption (Innovation): whether the use case enables a designer to produce functional code-based prototypes without engineering support - a workflow not previously accessible at this fidelity level - Cycle time compression (Velocity): elapsed time from interaction specification to testable prototype relative to engineering-supported prototype baseline; practitioner evidence also points to a reduction in implementation time as a proportion of the overall design workflow
named_tools: Claude (Artifacts), Claude Code, v0 (Vercel), Replit, Cursor
citations: [TBD - Source Registry not yet created] Sources: Cattapan, Chawla, Orego 3/5, Li et al., Borg et al., Batchu, Maxime, Wen (Anthropic)

name: Design Variants
description: The designer specifies design parameters - color palette, typography, layout direction, or component style - and an AI tool generates multiple visual variants simultaneously. The designer reviews the variant set, identifies which directions meet brand, accessibility, and design system requirements, and selects options for further development or stakeholder review. Approver arrangement: AI produces the variant array autonomously; the designer's role is gating - curating and advancing rather than co-generating. Unlike AI coding tools, which commit to a single implementation direction, design-tool-based variant generation supports non-linear exploration - enabling comparison across fundamentally different directions before any commitment is made.

value_hypothesis.sentence: Compresses the time required to explore a design space by generating multiple parallel variants simultaneously, enabling evaluation of more options than sequential manual exploration allows.

value_indicator: - Iteration speed (Velocity): number of distinct design variants evaluable in a fixed time window relative to manual exploration baseline - Scale expansion (Innovation): whether the use case enables evaluation of variant sets at a scale not feasible through manual production
citations: [TBD - Source Registry not yet created] Sources: NNg Tools, Noltenius, Wen (Anthropic)

name: Placeholder and First Draft Content Generation
description: During wireframing and prototyping, the designer prompts AI to generate two categories of content: placeholder imagery to populate layout compositions, and first-draft UX copy - microcopy, labels, error messages, onboarding text - to test messaging and information architecture before final content design. AI generates at volume; the designer reviews for contextual fit, demographic appropriateness, and tonal alignment, then gates what enters the prototype. This is content produced to test, not to ship - the distinction from AI-assisted copy generation in the Deliver phase (UC DEL-044), where content is refined for production. This UC is expected to evolve as two conditions mature: design systems become more deeply integrated into AI generation workflows, and those design systems incorporate content design guidance - voice principles, vocabulary constraints, tone frameworks. When both conditions are met, AI-generated first-draft content could become sufficiently scaffolded to reduce the Approver review burden and shift the arrangement toward Co-creator.

value_hypothesis.sentence: Populates design mockups with contextually appropriate placeholder imagery and first-draft copy faster than manual approaches, producing higher-fidelity prototypes that support more realistic stakeholder review and user testing.

value_indicator: - Cycle time compression (Velocity): time to populate a prototype with contextually relevant content relative to manual or Lorem ipsum baseline - Expert assessment delta (Quality): assessed realism and contextual fit of AI-generated content vs. Lorem ipsum or manual first-draft baseline
citations: [TBD - Source Registry not yet created] Sources: NNg Tools, Fanny, Noltenius

name: Prompt Templates and Instruction File Development
description: The designer builds organisational AI infrastructure - instruction files, system prompts, and prompt templates that standardise how team members interact with AI tools. This shifts quality management from reactive to preventive: teams encode design system rules, heuristics, and quality standards into the generation context before work begins, rather than correcting AI output after the fact. Director arrangement: the human sets the constraints the instructions must encode; AI drafts and iterates but cannot supply organisational reasoning. Output is not a project artifact but a reusable organisational asset that governs future AI-assisted work.

value_hypothesis.sentence: Builds organisational AI capability through reusable prompt templates and instruction files that reduce variance in AI output quality across team members and compress the time practitioners spend learning to prompt effectively.

value_indicator: - Knowledge asset creation (Learning): whether the use case produces reusable artifacts - templates, prompt libraries, instruction files - that accumulate as durable team capability - Practice diffusion (Learning): rate at which AI-enabled practices spread across the team once standardised templates are in place
citations: [TBD - Source Registry not yet created] Sources: Chawla (Atlassian), Wen (Anthropic), Newman

name: Personalized Research Stimulus Generation
description: The researcher designs a stimulus framework for participant profiles, then works with AI to generate personalized versions of the materials shown during research - mockups, scenarios, vignettes, or concept descriptions - tailored to each participant's profile, role, use case, or proficiency level. Novice users get simpler interfaces with guidance; expert users see more complex versions with advanced features. Stimuli can be adapted across markets or segments, offering imagery, scenarios, and interaction patterns to fit expectations. The researcher evaluates whether AI-generated stimuli are realistic, non-leading, and appropriately scoped; AI generates and adapts content at scale. An extension of this use case is the "real-time hybrid session", where personalized concept variants are generated mid-interview based on what a participant has described - enabling simultaneous validation and directional feedback in a single session. This spans two phases: stimuli are designed during Concept and deployed during Validate.

value_hypothesis.sentence: Removes the constraint of "we can only test what we can build" by enabling personalised stimuli tailored to each participant's role, industry, proficiency level, or market without proportional increases in design or engineering time.

value_indicator: - Scale expansion (Innovation): whether the use case enables stimulus personalisation across participant volumes, proficiency levels, or markets not feasible through manual production - Expert assessment delta (Quality): assessed realism and contextual fit of AI-generated stimuli vs. generic stimuli baseline as rated by participants or independent reviewers
citations: [TBD - Source Registry not yet created] Sources: Orego 3/5

name: Custom Figma Plugin Development
description: The designer identifies a repetitive or specialised task in their Figma workflow that no existing plugin addresses, specifies the desired functionality in natural language, and uses an AI coding assistant to generate plugin code. The designer tests the output, identifies gaps or errors, and re-prompts to refine. Unlike professional plugin development requiring JavaScript expertise, this approach makes custom workflow tooling accessible to designers without an engineering background. Director arrangement: the designer holds the workflow context and requirements the plugin must serve; AI generates the implementation without the designer needing to understand the underlying code. Output is not a design artifact but a persistent workflow tool - a reusable automation that extends beyond any single project. This UC spans two phases: the plugin is specified and built during Concept; it is deployed and maintained during Deliver.

value_hypothesis.sentence: Enables designers to build bespoke workflow automation without engineering support, creating custom tooling for repetitive or specialised tasks not covered by existing plugins.

value_indicator: - Automation rate (Efficiency): proportion of target task steps handled by the plugin without manual intervention - New method adoption (Innovation): whether the use case enables workflow automation not previously accessible to the design team without engineering support
citations: [TBD - Source Registry not yet created] Sources: Chawla (Tesco)

name: Moodboarding and Visual Language Exploration
description: The designer prompts an image generation model to produce visual references, moodboard components, and aesthetic explorations during the concept phase. Designer and AI iterate: the designer refines prompts, evaluates outputs for brand fit and directional validity, and selects images that articulate visual language directions to team members and stakeholders. Unlike stock photography or manual illustration, AI image generation enables exploration of highly specific visual combinations not available in reference libraries. The Co-creator arrangement reflects the iterative dynamic: aesthetic judgments made by the designer shape subsequent prompts; AI generates; the designer selects and redirects.

value_hypothesis.sentence: Enables exploration of visual language directions at a specificity and volume not achievable through stock photography or manual illustration, compressing the time from brief to visual direction shortlist.

value_indicator: - Scale expansion (Innovation): whether the use case enables exploration of visual combinations not available in reference libraries or stock photography - Iteration speed (Velocity): number of distinct visual language directions evaluable in a fixed time window relative to manual moodboarding baseline
named_tools: Midjourney, DALL-E (OpenAI), Adobe Firefly, Stable Diffusion
citations: [TBD - Source Registry not yet created] Sources: Babich, Noltenius

name: System-Grounded Drafting
description: The designer connects an AI generation tool to the design system via a context protocol (e.g., MCP), enabling the AI to query available components, tokens, spacing rules, and brand constraints before generating artifacts. This connection is the enabling move: without it, AI generates from generic training data and every output requires manual correction against the system. With it, the AI drafts layouts, component compositions, and design explorations that start closer to system-compliant, compressing the gap between first draft and usable artifact. Director arrangement: the designer establishes the system connection and defines generation parameters; AI generates outputs the designer evaluates for genuine system conformance versus surface resemblance. The output is exploratory - concept-phase drafts that accelerate design exploration, not production-ready code. For production- scale generation with deterministic output and near- zero hallucination rates, see DEL-052.

value_hypothesis.sentence: Reduces the gap between AI-generated artifacts and production-ready outputs by grounding generation in the team's actual design system, compressing the manual correction cycle that standard AI generation requires.

value_indicator: - Compliance pass rate (Quality): proportion of AI-generated outputs conforming to design system constraints without manual correction, relative to non-system-aware generation baseline - Effort reduction (Efficiency): reduction in manual correction time required to bring AI-generated artifacts into design system conformance
named_tools: Figma AI (MCP integration), Claude (MCP), Cursor (MCP + design tokens)
citations: [TBD - Source Registry not yet created] Sources: Lykoudi, Newman, Figma/Boyer, Chawla, Huba, Morales Achiardi, Maxime, Wen (Anthropic)

name: Scenario Generation
description: The designer provides a design challenge, persona sketch, or contextual brief, and works with AI to generate narrative design scenarios - stories describing how a user might interact with a proposed design in a real-world context. Scenarios help the team evaluate whether a concept holds up under realistic use conditions and communicate design intent to stakeholders. The Co-creator arrangement reflects iterative back-and-forth: the designer provides contextual grounding and evaluates whether scenarios are realistic, appropriately scoped, and generative for design decisions; AI drafts and elaborates narrative content at speed.

value_hypothesis.sentence: Accelerates scenario generation during concept development, enabling teams to evaluate design directions against a wider range of use contexts than manual authoring allows.

value_indicator: - Cycle time compression (Velocity): time to scenario set from brief relative to manual authoring baseline - Expert assessment delta (Quality): assessed realism and generative value of AI-generated scenarios vs. manually authored baseline
citations: [TBD - Source Registry not yet created] Sources: Muehlhaus

name: Concept Critique
description: The designer presents a design concept - wireframes, mockups, or narrative descriptions - and prompts AI to evaluate it against specified criteria: user needs, design principles, consistency requirements, or competitive benchmarks. AI produces a structured critique; the designer evaluates each finding, accepting valid challenges, redirecting misapplied ones, and using the assessment as a prompt for design refinement. Challenger arrangement: AI proposes critical observations about the concept; the designer interrogates those observations rather than producing self-critique.

value_hypothesis.sentence: Provides structured external critique of design concepts at low cost, surfacing issues the designer may be too close to identify while compressing time to design review feedback.

value_indicator: - Expert assessment delta (Quality): proportion of AI-identified critique points subsequently validated by human design review - Cycle time compression (Velocity): time from design concept to structured critique relative to peer review or design critique session baseline
citations: [TBD - Source Registry not yet created] Sources: Muehlhaus, Wen (Anthropic)

name: Design Remix
description: The designer supplies visual reference material - existing design concepts, style samples, layout variations, or idea sketches - and prompts AI to manipulate and fuse them to meet specific design requirements. AI generates new creative approaches from the inputs; the output is rough by design, a starting point rather than a resolved proposal. The designer keeps what matches their intent, discards the rest, and feeds refined selections or reframed elements back into the loop.

value_hypothesis.sentence: Enables designers to generate directionally new concepts by systematically recombining elements from disparate existing designs, expanding the explorable solution space beyond what manual iteration can reach in equivalent time.

value_indicator: - Output novelty (Innovation): whether AI-fused concepts produce directional combinations meaningfully distinct from any single source input - Iteration speed (Velocity): number of concept remix directions explored per session relative to manual recombination baseline
citations: [TBD - Source Registry not yet created] Sources: Muehlhaus

name: Research Preview
description: The team generates a functional product build before finalising visual design, and gets it in front of real users as early as possible - through a limited pilot, an internal preview, or a framed early release - to test functionality before investing in polish. Being explicit about the work's early state, and committing visibly to iterate on what users report, allows participants to engage as collaborators rather than judges of a finished product. The approach inverts the traditional design-then-build sequence: functionality is validated first, visual refinement follows. AI- assisted prototyping makes the inversion economically viable - a working build takes hours rather than weeks to generate, and the iteration loop is tight enough that feedback translates quickly into shipped improvements.

value_hypothesis.sentence: Compresses the concept-to-user-feedback cycle by shipping functional builds before visual polish, enabling iteration on real user behaviour while building product trust through demonstrated responsiveness rather than upfront quality.

value_indicator: - Cycle time compression (Velocity): elapsed time from concept to first user feedback on deployed functionality relative to traditional design-then-build baseline - New method adoption (Innovation): whether the workflow enables a validation cadence not previously feasible within typical design and engineering resource constraints
citations: [TBD - Source Registry not yet created] Sources: Maxime, Wen (Anthropic)

name: Cross-functional First Drafts
description: Non-designers - product managers, researchers, and engineers - use prompt-to-UI or -prototype as a "first draft" communication tool for expressing intent, but not as a design output. PMs include these mock-ups in PRDs to reduce requirement ambiguity; researchers to communicate research proposals to stakeholders; engineers scope implementation before coding begins. The value is shared understanding across functions, not a design artifact intended to progress into production. Co-creator arrangement: the non-designer stakeholder supplies intent and context; AI generates visual form until the communication purpose is served.

value_hypothesis.sentence: Gives non-designer roles a visual communication channel for product intent that previously required design tool proficiency; reduces requirement ambiguity in PRDs, research proposals, and implementation scoping by replacing text descriptions with generated screens.

value_indicator: - Alignment quality (Quality): self-reported reduction in requirement ambiguity or misalignment relative to text-only communication baseline - New method adoption (Innovation): whether non-designer roles gain a visual communication channel previously accessible only to practitioners with design tool proficiency
named_tools: Figma AI, v0 (Vercel), Lovable, Galileo AI
citations: [TBD - Source Registry not yet created] Sources: GenUI study (Chen/Knearem/Li, arXiv 2025)

name: Prompted UI Tech Scaffolding
description: Software engineers generate low-fidelity visual scaffolding - rough screen layouts that help define UI surface before coding begins. The output is a technical reference artifact, not a design proposal: sufficient to scope implementation and support early discussion, not to represent design intent. This use case is distinct from designer-led vibe coding (CON-021): the developer drives, required fidelity is low, and the artifact serves the engineer's own planning rather than stakeholder evaluation. Director arrangement: the developer specifies what they need to advance; AI generates a working reference; the developer uses or discards without iterative refinement.

value_hypothesis.sentence: Enables software engineers to generate low-fidelity UI scaffolding for implementation scoping without designer dependency, bootstrapping the visual reference needed to start coding on small projects or low-stakes features.

value_indicator: - Cycle time compression (Velocity): elapsed time from feature requirements to implementation start relative to waiting for designer-produced spec baseline - New method adoption (Innovation): whether developers gain a visual scoping capability that removes designer dependency for low-stakes or small-project prototyping
citations: [TBD - Source Registry not yet created] Sources: GenUI study (Chen/Knearem/Li, arXiv 2025)

name: Thematic Coding of Interview Data
description: The researcher uploads interview transcripts and works iteratively with an LLM to identify themes, cluster codes, and surface patterns. AI proposes initial codes and groupings; the researcher evaluates, reframes, merges, and challenges. The researcher holds interpretive authority - AI accelerates the mechanical work of tagging and cross- referencing while the researcher drives the analytical framework. Evidence points to a practitioner gap: those without deep qualitative research experience tend to overestimate the reliability of AI- generated codes, while experienced researchers apply appropriate caution. Practitioner background is therefore a significant variable in how safely this UC is executed.

value_hypothesis.sentence: Compresses thematic coding of qualitative interview data from days to hours while surfacing cross-participant patterns a solo researcher might miss.

value_indicator: - Cycle time compression (Velocity): elapsed time from raw transcripts to coded theme map relative to fully manual baseline - Synthesis coverage (Insight): proportion of transcript data incorporated into findings relative to manual coding baseline
named_tools: Claude (long-context transcript analysis), Dovetail (AI-assisted tagging), Atlas.ti (AI coding features), ChatGPT
citations: [TBD - Source Registry not yet created] Sources: Nishat, Orego (4/5), Schenk, Maleki (Microsoft), CAILA, Candello et al.

name: Problem Statement Drafting
description: The designer or researcher provides research findings or synthesis outputs to an LLM and asks it to draft problem statements framing the design challenge. AI generates candidate statements; the practitioner evaluates for accuracy, focus, and actionability before selecting or refining. Evidence indicates AI-generated statements tend to be saturated with unrequested information - elaborating beyond what the format requires - making human editing and focusing an essential step, not an optional one.

value_hypothesis.sentence: Generates candidate problem statements from synthesis inputs, reducing blank- page effort while the designer applies judgment on focus, scope, and connection to the actual design opportunity.

value_indicator: - Cycle time compression (Velocity): elapsed time from synthesis to approved problem statement - Statement clarity (Quality): independent rating of statement actionability relative to a researcher-only drafting baseline
citations: [TBD - Source Registry not yet created] Sources: Cattapan (Shopify), Noltenius

name: Persona Generation
description: The designer provides research synthesis, user data, or participant profiles and asks an LLM to generate candidate personas. AI proposes persona structures, attributes, and narratives; the designer evaluates for representativeness, relevance, and grounding in actual research. Evidence indicates output quality improves dramatically with structured prompts and meta-prompting - generic prompts yield generic personas. The human's primary role is to challenge AI-generated personas against actual research data and reject those that don't hold up.

value_hypothesis.sentence: Rapidly generates multiple persona candidates for designer evaluation, with output quality scaling significantly with prompt structure and specificity.

value_indicator: - Candidate generation speed (Velocity): time from synthesis inputs to reviewable persona set - Research groundedness (Quality): proportion of persona attributes traceable to actual participant data rather than AI extrapolation
citations: [TBD - Source Registry not yet created] Sources: Figma (7 UCs), Orego (1/5), Muehlhaus

name: Journey Mapping
description: The designer provides interview transcripts, observation notes, or synthesis outputs to an AI-powered tool that generates a draft journey map - identifying stages, steps, actions, emotions, and pain points. AI structures the raw data into journey format; the designer evaluates, reorganises stages, merges steps, and applies interpretive judgment about what the journey actually reveals. Current evidence is based on a purpose-built research prototype; production-ready tools for this workflow are not yet established.

value_hypothesis.sentence: Transforms interview data into a structured journey map significantly faster than manual methods, enabling teams to move from raw data to shared understanding more quickly.

value_indicator: - Cycle time compression (Velocity): elapsed time from interview data to reviewable journey map draft - Cross-participant pattern coverage (Insight): proportion of recurring patterns surfaced relative to a researcher-only baseline
citations: [TBD - Source Registry not yet created; flag for additional citation search - single source only] Sources: GeneyMAP (CHI 2025)

name: Design Brief Creation
description: The designer provides project context - client goals, research outputs, constraints, and requirements - and works iteratively with an LLM to draft a design brief. AI generates structured sections covering problem context, design goals, constraints, and success criteria; the designer evaluates, refines, and ensures the brief accurately captures the project's intent. This is one of the few UCs in the catalog supported by quantitative evidence of time reduction from a controlled study.

value_hypothesis.sentence: Reduces design brief creation time significantly (48.6% reduction documented) while maintaining the strategic clarity that makes briefs useful for guiding design decisions.

value_indicator: - Cycle time compression (Velocity): elapsed time from project inputs to approved design brief; 48.6% reduction documented - Brief completeness (Efficiency): proportion of sections completed without major rework relative to a from-scratch baseline
citations: [TBD - Source Registry not yet created] Sources: Zhu et al. (Frontiers 2024)

name: Survey & Open-Text Analysis
description: The researcher exports open-text survey responses and runs them through an LLM or AI analytics tool to cluster responses, extract themes, and surface patterns at scale. AI performs the bulk of analytical processing autonomously; the researcher reviews output - validating theme labels, checking cluster coherence, and interpreting what patterns mean for design decisions. This UC is particularly valuable for large datasets where manual open-text coding would be prohibitively time-consuming.

value_hypothesis.sentence: Enables analysis of open- text survey data at a scale manual coding cannot sustain, surfacing themes and patterns across large response sets without proportional researcher time.

value_indicator: - Response throughput (Efficiency): open-text responses processable per researcher-hour relative to manual coding baseline - Theme coverage (Insight): proportion of meaningful response content captured in identified themes relative to a manual coding baseline
citations: [TBD - Source Registry not yet created] Sources: Orego (4/5), Nishat

name: Session Summarization & Rapid Debrief
description: After a research session, an AI tool transcribes the recording and generates a structured summary - including key quotes, themes, notable moments, and suggested highlights. The researcher reviews for accuracy and completeness before sharing with stakeholders. The primary value is speed: same-day summaries enable alignment while sessions are still fresh, without requiring the researcher to manually review and write up full session notes.

value_hypothesis.sentence: Enables same-day session summaries for stakeholder sharing, compressing the time between fieldwork and team alignment while reducing the manual burden of post-session writeup.

value_indicator: - Turnaround time (Velocity): elapsed time from session end to shareable summary relative to manual writeup baseline - Researcher time per session (Efficiency): researcher time spent on summarisation relative to fully manual review baseline
citations: [TBD - Source Registry not yet created] Sources: Maleki (Microsoft), Orego (4/5), Schenk, Maze

name: Project Knowledge Base Creation
description: The designer creates a Project Knowledge Base - an ad hoc but intentionally cultivated collection of project-specific materials (design system rules, heuristics, principles, prior research, user feedback) loaded into a context the designer can query on demand. This is a project-scoped working intelligence layer, not a systematic cross-project repository: it grows through deliberate human curation rather than automated ingestion, and is distinct from the organisation-wide Research Repository covered in IMP-059. Director arrangement: the human decides what belongs, how it is structured, and what constitutes signal versus noise; AI enables retrieval and cross-referencing across the full corpus. Ongoing enrichment with validation and testing outcomes is covered in IMP-065.

value_hypothesis.sentence: Transforms accumulated project knowledge into a queryable intelligence layer, enabling teams to surface relevant precedent, constraints, and insights on demand rather than through manual document search.

value_indicator: - Query response time (Efficiency): time to retrieve relevant precedent or constraint relative to manual document search baseline - Knowledge utilization rate (Innovation): proportion of accumulated project knowledge actively surfaced and applied to design decisions
citations: [TBD - Source Registry not yet created] Sources: Cattapan (Shopify), Newman

name: Cross-Study Meta-Analysis
description: The researcher provides findings, reports, or synthesis outputs from multiple studies conducted over time, and asks an LLM to identify longitudinal patterns, recurring themes, contradictions, and emergent signals across the corpus. AI proposes cross-study connections and patterns; the researcher evaluates whether they reflect genuine longitudinal trends or are artifacts of sampling differences, study design variation, or AI pattern-matching on superficial features. The value is in surfacing meta-level insight that is difficult to achieve through manual comparison of individual study reports.

value_hypothesis.sentence: Surfaces longitudinal patterns and cross-study signals from accumulated research that would be impractical to identify through manual comparison of individual study outputs.

value_indicator: - Pattern discovery rate (Insight): number of actionable cross-study patterns identified relative to a manual meta-analysis baseline - Analysis time (Efficiency): elapsed time from research corpus to meta-analysis output relative to manual comparison baseline
citations: [TBD - Source Registry not yet created; flag for additional citation search - single source only] Sources: Orego (5/5)

name: Layer Renaming
description: AI scans the layer tree of a design file and renames layers according to a target convention - replacing default names ("Frame 247", "Rectangle 12") with semantically meaningful labels that reflect each layer's content and role. The designer triggers the operation, reviews the results, and corrects any misattributions. Auditor arrangement: AI processes the full layer tree without step-by-step direction; the designer spot-checks output and intervenes on exceptions. The value is straightforward efficiency - manual layer renaming is tedious work that designers routinely skip under deadline pressure, resulting in illegible files at handoff. Automating the task makes clean file hygiene the default rather than the exception.

value_hypothesis.sentence: Automates the tedious manual cleanup of design file layer names, making consistent naming the default rather than a task designers skip under deadline pressure.

value_indicator: - Cleanup time eliminated (Efficiency): time previously spent on manual layer renaming per file or per sprint - Naming consistency (Quality): proportion of layers following team naming conventions at handoff, relative to manual baseline
named_tools: Figma (Rename It plugin, native AI features), Sketch (Rename It)
citations: [TBD - Source Registry not yet created] Sources: NNg Tools survey, Noltenius

name: Copy Generation and Rewriting
description: AI generates or rewrites production UI copy - button labels, error messages, empty states, tooltips, onboarding steps - based on context, target reading level, and tone guidelines. At the basic level, a designer provides screen context and AI produces candidate strings for review. The more mature practice embeds content rules into the AI's operating context: copy type classifications (informative vs. persuasive vs. transactional), tone constraints by context (error messages are direct and humourless; marketing surfaces are warmer), vocabulary boundaries, and locale-specific register shifts. Practitioner evidence describes teams building custom GPTs with brand voice guidelines, UX copywriting rules, and copy type logic, then using the AI to produce tone-and-content compliance feedback for screen-by-screen review. This points toward content operating as a structured layer within the design system rather than freeform text composed per screen. Approver arrangement: AI drafts at volume; the human reviews for brand voice, contextual fit, and interaction-level clarity before copy enters production.

value_hypothesis.sentence: Compresses the cycle of drafting and refining production UI copy so that screens ship with considered microcopy rather than placeholder text that never gets replaced.

value_indicator: - Copy cycle time (Velocity): elapsed time from copy request to approved production string - Placeholder elimination (Quality): proportion of screens shipping with reviewed microcopy vs. placeholder or default text
named_tools: ChatGPT (custom GPTs with brand rules), Claude, Figma AI (text generation), Writer (brand voice), Blaze
citations: [TBD - Source Registry not yet created] Sources: NNg Tools survey, Noltenius, Lykoudi

name: Asset Discovery Across Files
description: AI searches across design files to locate assets that match a designer's intent - similar components, matching styles, existing patterns that could be reused or adapted rather than rebuilt. The designer describes what they need; AI returns candidates ranked by similarity. Approver arrangement: AI performs the search and surfaces candidates; the designer evaluates relevance and decides whether to reuse, adapt, or create new. The value is efficiency in large projects where the asset inventory has grown beyond what any single designer can hold in memory - especially in multi-team environments where components may exist in files the designer has never opened.

value_hypothesis.sentence: Reduces the time designers spend manually searching for existing assets across large multi-file projects, surfacing reusable components, styles, and patterns that would otherwise be recreated from scratch.

value_indicator: - Search time reduction (Efficiency): time spent locating existing assets relative to manual file-by-file search baseline - Duplication avoidance (Efficiency): proportion of new asset creation avoided through discovery of existing reusable components
citations: [TBD - Source Registry not yet created] Sources: NNg Tools survey

name: Design-to-Development Handoff
description: AI translates completed design artifacts into developer-consumable outputs - component markup, implementation specs, annotated code snippets, or living demo pages. The enabling architecture connects AI to both the design file and the target codebase via context protocols (e.g., MCP), so the AI maps design elements to existing components and generates output that fits the team's actual code patterns rather than producing generic code from visual inspection alone. Practitioner evidence describes pipelines from design tool to codebase mapping to rules-file-governed generation, producing specification documents and living demos directly from design artifacts. The handoff gap is expensive - industry estimates put the annual cost of translation friction at six figures per product pod. Approver arrangement: AI generates handoff artifacts; the designer validates design intent and the developer validates implementation feasibility before code enters the build.

value_hypothesis.sentence: Compresses the translation cycle between finished design and developer-ready specification, reducing both elapsed handoff time and the interpretation errors that accumulate when developers reconstruct design intent from static artifacts.

value_indicator: - Handoff cycle time (Velocity): elapsed time from design completion to developer-ready specification - Translation error rate (Risk Reduction): proportion of design-intent misinterpretations caught during development relative to manual handoff baseline
named_tools: Figma (MCP, Code Connect), Claude Code, Cursor, Builder.io, Supernova
